reasoning: In April 2025, President Zelenskyy signed a law extending martial law and general mobilization from May 9 until August 6, 2025. Under the Ukrainian constitution, national elections cannot legally be held while martial law is in effect. Although there is international pressure and tentative talks regarding a 30-day ceasefire as of mid-May 2025, no formal peace agreement exists, and active fighting continues. Moreover, the Ukrainian government has officially denied rumors of preparing for a summer election. Given that martial law currently extends past the July 1, 2025 deadline, and considering the lengthy legal and logistical processes required to lift martial law and schedule an election, it is extremely unlikely that the official announcement of a specific presidential election date will occur before July 1.
